title: promise-returning (async) hooks
manager: Let a hook return a promise the machine awaits before continuing, so callbacks can do real asynchronous work — a network call, a disk read — inside a step instead of being forced to be synchronous. This is what makes hooks usable for the I/O they usually need to do, with the machine's stepping model accounting for the wait rather than racing past it.
cs: async hooks must reconcile with the v11 transactional step boundary (emit buffers flush at commit); the design crux is await/ordering semantics inside a transaction and what a rejected promise does to the in-flight step; sits over the uniform hook registry (W6.77).
